terms.definition: In feature engineering, the step of choosing which raw input columns proceed to model training and inference, typically inside a reproducible preprocessing pipeline.
Fitting feature selectors using only training-fold data so validation and test labels never influence which columns are kept.
A boolean array indicating which input columns a fitted selector retained; used to align batch training with online scoring.
An sklearn transformer that keeps features whose importances or coefficients from a fitted base estimator exceed a threshold.
Recursive Feature Elimination with cross-validation to choose the number of features to keep without tuning k on a held-out test set.
The requirement that inference applies the same preprocessing and column subset that training used, usually by deploying a serialized Pipeline.
